title: How to Budget With Inconsistent Income
subtitle: The Floor-First method, explained.

intro.voiceText: Most budgeting advice was built for steady paychecks. This is for everyone else.

segments.voiceText: If you are a freelancer, a creator, or a business owner, your income does not arrive on the first and the fifteenth. It lands when it lands. And every budgeting app on the market assumes the opposite.
segments.headline:
Most apps assume
a {paycheck.}
segments.subhead: Yours is variable. The advice was wrong.

segments.voiceText: Floor-First Budgeting starts with one number. Your floor. That's bills plus tax. The amount you can not miss this month, no matter how the income lands.
segments.headline:
Know your
{floor.}
segments.subhead: Bills + tax. The amount you can't miss.

segments.voiceText: Every deposit fills the floor first. Not month by month. Deposit by deposit. The moment money lands, it gets split. Tax off the top. Bills reserved. Then the rest.
segments.headline:
Every deposit
fills the {floor first.}
segments.subhead: Not month by month. Deposit by deposit.

segments.voiceText: Slow months happen. Build your reserve before you spend, and slow months get paid by the reserve, not by next month's panic.
segments.headline:
Build your {reserve}
before you spend.
segments.subhead: Slow months get paid by the reserve.

segments.voiceText: When next month's floor is already reserved before next month begins, you have arrived. Most people call that moment relief. We call it being Able.
segments.headline:
One month ahead
= {Able.}
segments.subhead: When next month's floor is already reserved.

segments.voiceText: And at the end of the month, score reality. Not the plan. The month is judged on what happened, not what you intended.
segments.headline:
Score reality.
Not the {plan.}
segments.subhead: Judged on what happened. Not what you intended.

outro.voiceText: From Unable. To Able. Try it free for thirty days at becomeable.app.
